rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondents Tracy Dearman And Fusion Real Estate Network For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Tentative Ruling: Part 1 of 2 Ths tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Decedent Genette Trammell executed The Genette Trammell Living Trust UDT February 28, 2004 ("2004 Trust"). Just over a decade later, Genette executed a will ("2015 Will"), which revoked all prior wills and trusts. Genette died in late 2017. She owned real property known as 64 Potomac Street. Eventually, in PTR-18-302129, competing petitions for probate were filed. On March 19, 2021, and after a four-day trial, the court found the 2015 Will valid, and it ruled that the 2015 Will effectively revoked the related trust. The court appointed Floyd Trammell as executor. The 2015 Will left everything, including 64 Potomac Street, to Genette's disabled adult child, Donald Trammell. In the meantime, Lawrence Porchia, acting as trustee of the revoked 2004 Trust and in disregard of the 2015 Will, sold 64 Potomac Street to third parties, allegedly with the help of others, including Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network, Inc. Pending before the court is a petition by executor Floyd Trammell to recover funds related to the sale; Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network, Inc. are two of several respondents on that petition. Several of the parties to the petition participated in a mediation. The executor and Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network, Inc. reached a settlement that is conditioned on a judicial good faith determination under Code of Civil Procedure 877.6 et. seq. Now, Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network, Inc. move for a for a good faith determination. The motion is unopposed. Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network, Inc's motion for a good faith settlement determination is GRANTED. On its own motion, the court takes judicial notice of the following pleadings from this action: (1) Notice of Partial Conditional Settlement filed August 30, 2022 by Floyd Trammell, Executor for the Estate; (2) First Amended Petition for Return of Estate Property (Prob. Code section 850), filed January 31, 2022 by Floyd Trammell, Executor for the Estate; (3) Petition of Vanguard Properties, Inc. and Kilby Stenkamp for Equitable Indemnity, Contribution and Declaratory Relief, filed October 13, 2021 by Respondents Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. (4) Petition for Return of Estate Property (Prob. Code section 850), filed September 21, 2021 by Floyd Trammell, Executor for the Estate; (5) Petition to Obtain Real and personal Property Under Probate Code Sections 850, 859, filed February 26, 2019 by Donald Trammell by and through his Guardian Ad Litem Clinton Killian. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, (a)(1) Any party to an action in which it is alleged that two or more parties are joint tortfeasors or co-obligors on a contract debt shall be entitled to a hearing on the issue of the good faith of a settlement entered into by the plaintiff or other claimant and one or more alleged tortfeasors or co-obligors, upon giving notice in the manner provided in subdivision (b) of Section 1005. []

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondents Kilby Stenkamp And Vanguard Properties, Inc., To Determine Good Faith Of Settlement (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Tentative Ruling: Part 2 of 2 (b) The issue of the good faith of a settlement may be determined by the court on the basis of affidavits served with the notice of hearing, and any counteraffidavits filed in response, or the court may, in its discretion, receive other evidence at the hearing. (c) A determination by the court that the settlement was made in good faith shall bar any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor from any further claims against the settling tortfeasor or co-obligor for equitable comparative contribution, or partial or comparative indemnity, based on comparative negligence or comparative fault. (d) The party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue. [] When a motion for determination of good faith settlement is uncontested, a "barebones" motion that sets forth the ground of good faith, accompanied by a declaration which sets forth a brief background of the case, is sufficient to support a good faith determination. The court need not engage in a weighing of the Tech-Bilt factors. (City of Grand Terrace v. Sup. Ct (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261, "[O]nly when the good faith nature of a settlement is disputed, it is incumbent upon the trial court to consider and weigh the Tech-Bilt factors. That is to say, when no one objects, the barebones motion which sets forth the ground of good faith, accompanied by a declaration which sets forth a brief background of the case is sufficient."; Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Here, Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. have filed a motion setting forth the grounds of good faith of this settlement (MPA at 7:18-8:15). The Declaration of Matthew L. Green, filed in support of the motion and signed under penalty of perjury, sets forth a brief background of the case and the settlement negotiations. On the basis of the record before it, the court determines the settlement was made in good faith. Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

Decedent Genette Trammell executed The Genette Trammell Living Trust UDT February 28, 2004 ("2004 Trust"). Just over a decade later, Genette executed a will ("2015 Will"), which revoked all prior wills and trusts. Genette died in late 2017. She owned real property known as 64 Potomac Street. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Under Code of Civil Procedure section 877.6, (a)(1) Any party to an action in which it is alleged that two or more parties are joint tortfeasors or co-obligors on a contract debt shall be entitled to a hearing on the issue of the good faith of a settlement entered into by the plaintiff or other claimant and one or more alleged tortfeasors or co-obligors, upon giving notice in the manner provided in subdivision (b) of Section 1005.
